overview: Azorius Tempo is a Standard archetype that leverages cheap creatures with relevant ETB effects to assert board control while delaying the opponent's turns. It combines efficient clocks like Voice of Victory with versatile instant-speed interaction to handle problematic permanents. The deck's win condition revolves around applying early pressure, resetting the board with Avatar's Wrath to reuse ETBs, and scaling into a late-game threat with Aang, Swift Savior and Abandoned Air Temple.
win_conditions: Aang, Swift Savior as a proactive bounce and late-game 5/5 threat
Voice of Victory as an efficient clock and interaction deterrent
Avatar's Wrath to exile all creatures and reuse ETB effects
Abandoned Air Temple to boost creature power for closing damage

keep_criteria: Hands with cheap ETB creatures and early interaction
Hands containing Enduring Curiosity or dual lands for mana consistency
Hands with a clear clock like Voice of Victory
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking one-drops or early board presence
Hands heavy on lands or late-game cards without early interaction
Hands without answers to aggressive early pressure

examples.reason: Provides early filtering, a relevant ETB creature, and mana consistency to deploy interaction on curve.

early_game: Deploy cheap one-drops like Spyglass Siren and Aven Interrupter to filter and delay opponent's plays.
Use instant-speed interaction like No More Lies and Tishana’s Tidebinder to disrupt key triggers or permanents.
mid_game: Apply pressure with Voice of Victory and Floodpits Drowner to remove blockers and deal damage.
Utilize Avatar's Wrath to clear the board and trigger ETB effects a second time, gaining tempo advantage.
late_game: Transform Aang, Swift Savior into a 5/5 to boost board presence and close out games.
Leverage Abandoned Air Temple for power boosts and sideboard in attrition tools like Enduring Innocence.

key_cards.name: Spyglass Siren
key_cards.notes: Best blue one-drop; guarantees top filtering and cheap extra draws with Enduring Curiosity.

key_cards.name: Voice of Victory
key_cards.notes: Prevents opponent interaction on your turn; tokens count as extra draws on empty boards.

key_cards.name: Aang, Swift Savior
key_cards.notes: Functions as a proactive bounce and scales into a 5/5 that buffs all creatures in play.

key_cards.name: Avatar’s Wrath
key_cards.notes: Exiles all creatures to reuse ETB effects at a lower cost, preventing opponent recovery.

key_cards.name: Enduring Curiosity
key_cards.notes: Offers draw upon combat damage at instant speed, crucial for tempo decks.

key_cards.name: Abandoned Air Temple
key_cards.notes: Provides permanent power increase at end of opponent's turn, ideal for small-body creatures.

key_cards.name: Tishana’s Tidebinder
key_cards.notes: Permanently locks down Planeswalkers, Ouroboroid triggers, or Repurposing Bay.

key_cards.name: Floodpits Drowner
key_cards.notes: Removes blockers at end of opponent's turn while dealing two damage; can exile problematic creatures.

sequencing_tips: Play Floodpits Drowner at the end of the opponent's turn to remove blockers and deal damage simultaneously.
Use Aang, Swift Savior proactively as a bounce to disrupt opponent's turns before they resolve.
Cast Avatar's Wrath to exile all creatures, then replay ETB creatures to gain a second round of value.
Deploy Voice of Victory on your turn to pressure the opponent while protecting your board from interaction.
Hold Tishana’s Tidebinder for instant speed to permanently lock problematic triggers like Ouroboroid.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Disdainful Stroke for stack interaction against mana dorks like Quantum Riddler and Sab-Sunen, Luxa Embodied.
Bring in Spider-Sense to protect against removal and sweepers, and to resolve problematic creature triggers.
Sideboard Disdainful Stroke and Clarion Conqueror to handle artifacts, planeswalkers, and lock down their threats.
Use Ghost Vacuum for timely answers and Disdainful Stroke to disrupt their casting sequence.
Sideboard in Seam Rip to combat faster clocks and aggressive creature combinations.
Prepare for late-game plans with Ghost Vacuum against Stormchaser’s Talent variants and Izzet Prowess.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Azorius Tempo is an archetype that uses cheap creatures with relevant ETB effects to assert board dominance while delaying your opponent's turns, combined with cheap interaction to deal with the main troublesome cards.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Aang, Swift Savior as an interaction and win condition
Voice of Victory... offering an efficient clock
Avatar’s Wrath can turn games in our favor by allowing us to exile all creatures in play... use the ETB effect of the others a second time
Abandoned Air Temple guarantees a significant power boost in games where we are ahead on the clock

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Floodpits Drowner can be used... to remove a blocker from the board at the end of the opponent's turn while guaranteeing two more damage.
Aang also has the advantage of being able to be played proactively as a 'bounce'
Avatar’s Wrath... can be used as a way to reuse all the ETBs of your cards a second time
Voice of Victory... plays a fundamental role in pressuring the opponent while preventing certain types of interaction
Tishana’s Tidebinder is still the most versatile Stifle in Standard, capable of permanently 'locking down' Planeswalkers, the trigger for Ouroboroid

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Disdainful Stroke is primarily used against Jeskai Control, Reanimator, Omniscience, and Artifact lists... Simic versions... Quantum Riddler and Sab-Sunen... Spider-Sense... Ideal against Dimir Midrange... Seam Rip... Red Aggro, or the combination of Badgermole Cub and mana dorks... Ouroboroid... Stormchaser’s Talent decks... Izzet Prowess, Lessons and Dimir Bounce.
